findings.description: EligibleLifecycle cross-field guard is one-directional: __setattr__ validates source_status but `source` is plain-mutable, so reassigning source after construction leaves an out-of-domain source_status with no error; docstring says 'every assignment path' — latent convention-held gap, no live call site reassigns source.

RelationshipGroup.members is list[str] inside frozen dataclass — shallow-frozen only (group.members.append succeeds); not covered by the documented dict-bag deferral; fix mirrors BindingIdentity.secret_slots tuple pattern. Also members-nonempty and digest-agreement are producer-held invariants.

RelationshipGroup.consumer = members[0].consumer_skill under-represents multi-consumer groups: the consumer-roles branch classifies intentional_rerun_evidence precisely on >=2 distinct roles, yet the record attributes the group to the first member's role; meaning for n>1 underdetermined.
VerificationBaseline census bucket-key/row.source agreement unenforced (no __post_init__); consumers key on row.source so blast radius limited.

note: Two slightly-overstating comments ('nothing mutates them after construction', 'every assignment path') are the subjects of findings 1-2, verified not double-filed; no documented_falsehood.
